item.title: Gold Steadies After Advancing on Japan’s Yen Intervention
item.description: Gold held a gain after the US dollar retreated sharply on Japan’s intervention in the foreign-exchange market.

item.title: US Stock Futures Rise on Tech Earnings, Yen Gains: Markets Wrap
item.description: US equity-index futures opened higher after Wall Street climbed to fresh highs, buoyed by resilient US economic growth and solid corporate earnings, while oil retreated from a four-year peak. The yen gained on a report of Japan’s intervention.

item.title: Warsh Must Confront Risks of Dissenting in His First Fed Vote
item.description: When Kevin Warsh hosts his first meeting as the new chair of the Federal Reserve, he’ll face an immediate dilemma: Stare down White House demands for immediate interest-rate cuts, or stare down fellow policymakers who remain skeptical of the need to ease.

item.title: Oil Holds Weekly Gain as Trump Says Iran Blockade Is Working
item.description: Oil held its second weekly gain as US President Donald Trump said he was sticking with a naval blockade of Iranian ports, elevating concerns the vital Strait of Hormuz would not reopen anytime soon.

item.title: S&P Weighs New Index Rules to Speed Up Addition of Mega IPOs
item.description: S&P Dow Jones Indices LLC has launched a consultation that could eventually speed up the entry of mega cap companies seeking to IPO into its indexes, including the S&P 500.

item.title: First Brands Creditor Sues Auditor BDO Over Missed Red Flags
item.description: A lender that owns First Brands Corp. debt sued auditor BDO USA P.C., alleging it failed to detect problems at the auto parts supplier before it collapsed into bankruptcy and federal prosecutors accused founder Patrick James of fraud.

item.title: How the US Government Plans to Refund Billions in Tariffs
item.description: When the Supreme Court struck down President Trump’s tariffs, it triggered a $166 billion refund process. On today’s Big Take podcast: the logistics of that payout and how soon businesses can expect relief.

item.title: Trader Mercuria Sues Baltic Exchange Over Oil Shipping Benchmark
item.description: Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. is suing the Baltic Exchange over the alleged distortion of a key benchmark for the cost of shipping oil from the Middle East.

item.title: Air Canada Suspends 2026 Guidance Amid Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: Air Canada suspended its full-year 2026 guidance due to uncertainty related to jet fuel prices.

item.title: Venezuela Raises Minimum Wage to $240 Amid Rising Social Unrest
item.description: Venezuela more than doubled the minimum monthly wage to $240 as authorities try to ease pressure on households after months of high inflation and growing unrest.

item.title: Alphabet Has the Second Biggest One-Day Jump in Market Cap Ever
item.description: Alphabet Inc. added a historic amount to its market capitalization on Thursday, following blowout quarterly earnings that sent the stock skyrocketing.

item.title: Tanger Beats Earnings Estimates as Gen Z Drives Return to Physical Stores
item.description: Tanger Inc. posted first-quarter earnings that beat expectations and bumped up its full-year guidance, bolstered by robust tenant occupancy rates and a return to physical retail stores by Gen Z shoppers.

item.title: Apple Tops Estimates, Even as iPhone Sales Fail to Impress
item.description: Apple Inc. delivered second-quarter revenue that edged past analysts’ estimates, helped by a rebound in China, while failing to produce the kind of blowout results that some investors were anticipating.

item.title: Gresham House Said to Plan €1 Billion Fund for Battery Storage
item.description: Gresham House plans to raise a fund targeting roughly €1 billion ($1.2 billion) to give investors access to the growing market for battery-storage systems, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: AIG Profit Jumps as CEO Zaffino’s Strategic Deals Take Effect
item.description: American International Group Inc. reported first-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street expectations, driven in part by a jump in net premiums written and lower catastrophe losses.

item.title: Roblox Shares Tumble After User Numbers Fall Short
item.description: Roblox Corp. reported first-quarter users that fell short of analysts’ expectations after implementing safety features restricting how kids, who make up a majority of its audience, can use the platform.

item.title: Reddit Beats Sales Expectations Again on Continued Ads Growth
item.description: Reddit Inc. projected sales in the current period that surpassed Wall Street estimates, continuing a streak of strong revenue growth powered by the company’s surging advertising business.

item.title: Citi’s Bhatia Warns of Private Credit ‘Tourists’ Forced to Sell
item.description: Private credit rookies selling into a downturn poses a potential threat to corporate debt, Citigroup Inc.’s Mickey Bhatia warned.

item.title: Ares, JPMorgan Ink $800 Million Private Credit Deal for GoodLife
item.description: A group including Ares Management Corp. provided around $800 million in a private credit deal to finance Apollo Global Management Inc.’s investment in the GoodLife Group, which operates health clubs in Canada, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Trump Says He Doesn’t Care If Powell Remains on Fed Board
item.description: President Donald Trump said he did not care if the Federal Reserve’s Jay Powell remained as a governor after his tenure as the central bank’s chair ends.

item.title: Private Credit Giants Try to Reassure Investors on AI Risks to Software Bets
item.description: Three of the biggest names in private credit moved to reassure investors this week about the AI risks facing their software borrowers, deploying proprietary “score cards” and outside consultants.

item.title: How Big Tech’s AI Ambitions Are Fueling a Borrowing Boom
item.description: For the past few years, the largest US technology companies have been in a costly race to develop advanced artificial intelligence systems while at the same time providing computing power to a burgeoning field of startups. To chase these goals, they have radically changed how they finance their growth. Long reliant on rich revenues and share price increases, Alphabet Inc.’s Google, Meta Platforms Inc. and other tech giants now are borrowing heavily to build the technology that makes chatbots run

item.title: Fitch Warns US Debt Burden Is ‘Far Above’ Other AA Rated Nations
item.description: The US debt rating is being challenged by a widening deficit that leaves the country’s “debt burden far above” rival AA rated sovereigns, says Fitch Ratings.

item.title: Analysis of Apple Second-Quarter Earnings
item.description: An Apple store in New York. Photographer: Victor J. Blue/Bloomberg

item.title: Goldman’s Covello Says Buy AI Hyperscalers Over Chipmakers
item.description: Investors looking to profit amid the buildout boom in AI infrastructure should favor big-spending “hyperscalers” over chipmakers, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s Jim Covello.

item.title: NY Pied-à-Terre Tax Needs Significant Rates to Earn $500 Million
item.description: Luxury second homes in New York will have to be taxed at a “significant” amount to generate the $500 million of revenue the city is counting on to help close a gaping budget hole, according to a new study from New York City Comptroller Mark Levine’s office.

item.title: Zuckerberg, Cook Not Interested in Buying NFL’s Seattle Seahawks
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg and Tim Cook aren’t planning a bid for the NFL’s Seattle Seahawks.

item.title: News Organizations Push Back Against Web Archive Used For AI
item.description: Major news organizations, including CNN, NBC and USA Today, have joined an effort to curb the storage of their content in a web archive used by artificial intelligence companies for training chatbots.

item.title: US Senate Bans Members, Staff From Trading on Prediction Markets
item.description: The US Senate voted unanimously Thursday to ban its members and staff from trading on fast-growing prediction markets, highlighting concerns about insider trading on the platforms after a US soldier was accused of using classified information to profit from trades.

item.title: Trump Says US Removing Scotch Tariffs After UK King’s Visit
item.description: US President Donald Trump said he would be removing some whiskey tariffs following a visit from King Charles III, a move that delivers a major trade concession to the UK.
